task_completion.description: Verify that the genomics package directory and required files are created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file explorer or code editor showing the directory 'C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\Talon\user\genomics\' containing two files: 'genomics.py' and 'genomics.talon'.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'user.parse_nucleotides' action is defined to map nucleotide names to single-letter codes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In 'genomics.py', look for a function 'parse_nucleotides' that takes a text input, ignores non-nucleotide words, and maps 'adenine', 'cytosine', 'guanine', 'thymine', and 'uracil' to 'A', 'C', 'G', 'T', and 'U' respectively.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'user.type_sequence' action is defined to parse and insert the sequence.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In 'genomics.py', look for a function 'type_sequence' that calls the nucleotide parsing logic and inserts the resulting string (e.g., using 'actions.insert()').

task_completion.description: Verify the 'user.search_motif' action is defined with the correct file reading, length checking, and insertion logic.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In 'genomics.py', look for a function 'search_motif' that: 1) Checks if the parsed sequence length is < 4 and triggers a 'Motif too short' notification (e.g., using 'app.notify'). 2) Opens 'C:\workspace\data\reference.fasta'. 3) Ignores header lines (e.g., lines starting with '>') and strips line breaks. 4) Searches for the sequence and inserts it followed by '[FOUND]' if present, or '[MISSING]' if not.

task_completion.description: Verify the voice commands are correctly defined in the Talon file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In 'genomics.talon', look for the exact voice commands 'dictate sequence <user.text>:' and 'search motif <user.text>:' mapped to call 'user.type_sequence(text)' and 'user.search_motif(text)' respectively.

integrity.description: Verify the files are created in the correct Talon user directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check the file path in the editor or terminal to ensure it is exactly 'C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\Talon\user\genomics\' and not a mock or temporary workspace folder.

integrity.description: Verify the search logic dynamically reads the FASTA file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the Python code actually opens and reads the contents of 'reference.fasta' to perform a string search, rather than hardcoding specific sequences to return '[FOUND]' or '[MISSING]'.

integrity.description: Verify the Talon file uses correct syntax.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure 'genomics.talon' uses proper Talon syntax (e.g., command followed by a colon, with indented action calls on the next lines) rather than plain text descriptions.
